div1.entryFree:
Vigour, 1) force, strength: Ven. 953. Tp. I, 2, 485. Err. IV, 4, 81. LLL IV, 3, 308. John III, 1, 104. R2 I, 3, 71. Troil. II, 3, 257. III, 3, 172. Tit. IV, 2, 108.
2) efficacy, efficiency: never could the strumpet, with all her double v., art and nature, once stir my temper, Meas. II, 2, 184. with a sudden v. it doth posset and curd . . . the blood, Hml. I, 5, 68. to try the v. of them (compounds) Cymb. I, 5, 21.
